rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ZBS LAW, LLP RE: 555 4TH STREET UNIT 607
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Surplus Funds From Proceeds
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 23, 2021 line 1. NOTICE OF HEARING RE: SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS is continued to August 12, 2021. The Court will provide notice. Hearing will be required. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STORY RENTALS, INC. VS. BIANCA KRIEG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 23, 2021 line 8. DEFENDANT BIANCA KRIEG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift the burden on the first element of CC 1942.4. No admissible evidence is provided as to the dwelling substantially lacking affirmative standard [habitability] characteristics or is declared substandard. While the Court will admit into evidence the Notice of Violation for its existence, the contents of such notice are not admissible. If the contents were admissible, there is no authority provided for the proposition that the defects listed in the Notice of Violation are "substantial" as a matter of law within the meaning of CC 1942.4. Defendant further failed to cite authority for the proposition that failure to list the periods for which rent is due in the Notice invalidates such notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EMMANUEL KLU ET AL VS. ROBERTO RIVERA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 23, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANTS ROBERTO RIVERA AND LOURDES RIVERA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint properly states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Defendant raises multiple factual issues that are not appropriate on a demurrer and fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a 10-day notice to perform is a "notice to vacate" within the meaning of SFRO 37.9(c). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
